# Proxygate CLI Command Reference

Auto-generated from the CLI definitions: every command, argument, and flag. For workflows and worked examples, see the skill guide (SKILL.md).

## Global options

- `--gateway <url>` - Override gateway URL (default: from config)
- `--keypair <path>` - Path to Solana keypair JSON file (default: from config)
- `--api-key <key>` - Override API key (default: from config)
- `--json` - Machine-readable JSON output (for scripting)
- `--no-color` - Disable colored output

## Commands

### `proxygate init`

Initialize Proxygate (use `proxygate login` instead)

- `--gateway <url>` - Gateway URL
- `--keypair <path>` - Path to existing keypair file (any format)
- `--generate` - Generate a new keypair
- `--username <name>` - Username for the wallet (required: 3-32 chars, lowercase letters, digits, single dashes)
- `--email <email>` - Contact email for the wallet (sends a verification email)

### `proxygate getting-started`

Interactive setup guide (use `proxygate login` instead)

- `--gateway <url>` - Gateway URL
- `--keypair <path>` - Path to Solana keypair JSON file

### `proxygate balance`

Show your USDC vault balance (total, available, pending, cooldown)

### `proxygate pricing`

Browse available APIs, sellers, and pricing (no auth required)

- `-s, --service <name>` - Filter by service name (e.g., openai, anthropic)

### `proxygate usage`

View your API usage history with per-service summaries

- `-s, --service <name>` - Filter by service (e.g., openai, anthropic)
- `--from <date>` - Start date (ISO 8601, e.g., 2026-03-01)
- `--to <date>` - End date (ISO 8601)
- `-l, --limit <n>` - Number of entries to show (default: 20)

### `proxygate proxy <listing> <path>`

Send a proxied request to an upstream API through a seller listing

- `-d, --data <json>` - Request body as JSON string
- `-X, --method <method>` - HTTP method (default: POST if -d given, GET otherwise)
- `--stream` - Stream SSE response chunks to stdout
- `--shield <mode>` - Shield scanning: off (default), monitor, or strict (+$0.005/req)
- `--seller <strategy>` - Seller selection: popular (default), cheapest, best-rated, fastest

### `proxygate deposit`

Deposit USDC from your Solana wallet into your Proxygate vault

- `-a, --amount <lamports>` - Amount in USDC base units (1 USDC = 1,000,000 lamports)
- `--rpc <url>` - Solana RPC URL (default: mainnet-beta)
- `--legacy` - Force the self-paid deposit path (you pay the network fee in SOL)
- `-y, --yes` - Skip confirmation prompt
- `--dry-run` - Show what would happen without sending the transaction

### `proxygate withdraw`

Withdraw USDC from your vault back to your Solana wallet

- `-a, --amount <lamports>` - Amount in USDC base units (omit to withdraw all available)
- `--rpc <url>` - Solana RPC URL
- `-y, --yes` - Skip confirmation prompt
- `--dry-run` - Show what would happen without withdrawing

### `proxygate withdraw-confirm`

Confirm an on-chain withdrawal with the gateway (recovery tool)

- `--tx <signature>` - Solana transaction signature to confirm

### `proxygate listings`

Manage your seller listings (create, update, pause, delete, rotate keys)

#### `proxygate listings list`

List all your seller listings

- `--table` - Display in human-readable table format

#### `proxygate listings create`

Create a new seller listing

- `--non-interactive` - Use CLI flags instead of interactive prompts
- `--service-name <name>` - Service name
- `--base-url <url>` - Service base URL (https://...)
- `--auth-pattern <pattern>` - Auth pattern: none (public), bearer, header, query, basic, oauth2_cc
- `--credential <key>` - Seller API key / credential (for bearer/header/query/basic)
- `--header-name <name>` - Custom header name (for header auth pattern)
- `--query-param <name>` - Query parameter name (for query auth pattern)
- `--basic-user <user>` - Basic auth username
- `--oauth2-flow-type <type>` - OAuth2 flow: standard, google_jwt
- `--oauth2-token-url <url>` - OAuth2 token URL
- `--oauth2-scopes <scopes>` - OAuth2 scopes (space-separated)
- `--oauth2-client-id <id>` - OAuth2 client ID
- `--oauth2-client-secret <secret>` - OAuth2 client secret
- `--oauth2-service-account-json <path>` - Google service account JSON path
- `--total-rpm <n>` - Total RPM capacity
- `--reserved-rpm <n>` - Reserved RPM (for own use)
- `--price <n>` - Price per request in micro-USDC. 0 = free (pending admin approval, Phase 51.6); >=1000 = paid ($0.001 floor)
- `--categories <slugs>` - Category slugs (comma-separated)
- `--description <text>` - Listing description
- `--allowed-paths <paths>` - Allowed paths (comma-separated)
- `--endpoints <file>` - Path to JSON file containing EndpointSpec[]
- `--validation-endpoint <path>` - Validation endpoint path
- `--shield <on|off>` - Shield request scanning: $0.005/req from payout (default: off)
- `--docs <file>` - Path to OpenAPI spec (.yaml/.json) or markdown (.md) documentation
- `--type <type>` - Listing type: proxy, skill, product, dataset, service, connector (default: proxy)
- `--endpoint-url <url>` - Skill endpoint URL (required for --type skill)
- `--file-url <url>` - Product file URL (required for --type product)
- `--bulk-url <url>` - Dataset bulk data URL (required for --type dataset)
- `--webhook-url <url>` - Service webhook URL (required for --type service)
- `--relay-url <url>` - Connector relay URL (required for --type connector)
- `--relay-method <method>` - Service HTTP method: GET, POST, PUT (default: POST)
- `--platform <name>` - Connector platform: slack, notion, discord, github, custom
- `--skip-test` - Deprecated: all listings are now tested before activation
- `--free-endpoint <spec>` - Mark endpoint as free (repeatable). Format: "/path" or "/path:wallet-cap" (default cap 100/day).
- `--endpoint-price <spec>` - Per-endpoint price override (repeatable). Format: "/path=microUSDC", e.g. "/v1/data=5000".
- `--free-daily-cap-per-wallet <n>` - Listing-level per-wallet daily cap for free endpoints (overrides default 100). NULL/omit = use proxy default.
- `--free-daily-cap-global <n>` - Listing-level global daily cap for free endpoints. NULL/omit = unlimited.
- `--free` - Shortcut for --price 0. Listing enters Pending Approval until an admin sets free_listing_approved.

#### `proxygate listings update <id>`

Update a listing (capacity, pricing, categories, description, paths, free endpoints)

- `--total-rpm <n>` - Total RPM capacity
- `--reserved-rpm <n>` - Reserved RPM
- `--price <n>` - Price per request in micro-USDC (min 1000 = $0.001)
- `--categories <slugs>` - Category slugs (comma-separated)
- `--description <text>` - Listing description
- `--allowed-paths <paths>` - Allowed paths (comma-separated)
- `--endpoints <file>` - Path to JSON file containing EndpointSpec[]
- `--shield <on|off>` - Shield request scanning: $0.005/req from payout
- `--free-endpoint <spec>` - Mark endpoint as free (repeatable). Format: "/path" or "/path:wallet-cap".
- `--endpoint-price <spec>` - Per-endpoint price override (repeatable). Format: "/path=microUSDC".
- `--free-daily-cap-per-wallet <n>` - Listing-level per-wallet daily cap for free endpoints.
- `--free-daily-cap-global <n>` - Listing-level global daily cap for free endpoints.

#### `proxygate listings pause <id>`

Pause a listing (removes from marketplace routing)

#### `proxygate listings unpause <id>`

Unpause a listing (re-enables marketplace routing)

#### `proxygate listings delete <id>`

Delete a listing (removes API key from Secret Manager)

- `-y, --yes` - Skip confirmation prompt

#### `proxygate listings rotate-key <id>`

Rotate API key or OAuth2 credentials for a listing

- `--non-interactive` - Use CLI flags instead of interactive prompts
- `--credential <key>` - New API key / credential
- `--oauth2-flow-type <type>` - OAuth2 flow: standard, google_jwt
- `--oauth2-client-id <id>` - OAuth2 client ID
- `--oauth2-client-secret <secret>` - OAuth2 client secret
- `--oauth2-service-account-json <path>` - Google service account JSON path
- `--validation-endpoint <path>` - Validation endpoint path

#### `proxygate listings docs <id>`

API docs: compact index by default; drill into one unit (--operation/--type/--endpoint); --raw for the full schema

- `--raw` - Full spec/schema (GraphQL as compact SDL). Large - prefer -o to a file.
- `-o, --output <file>` - Write --raw to a file instead of stdout (keeps it out of your context)
- `--operation <name>` - GraphQL: one operation + its return type fields (one level)
- `--type <name>` - GraphQL: one type's fields (one level)
- `--endpoint <path>` - REST: one endpoint's params + body schema. Accepts "/path" (GET) or "POST /path".
- `--search <term>` - Filter the index by name/path
- `--limit <n>` - Max index rows (default 40)

#### `proxygate listings upload-docs <id> <file>`

Upload OpenAPI (.yaml/.json), GraphQL schema (.graphql/.gql or introspection JSON), or markdown (.md) docs for a listing

- `--type <type>` - Force doc type: openapi, graphql, or markdown (auto-detected from extension). Use --type graphql for an introspection .json file.

#### `proxygate listings headers`

Manage upstream headers for a listing

##### `proxygate listings headers list <listing-id>`

List upstream headers

##### `proxygate listings headers set <listing-id> <name> <value>`

Set an upstream header (template vars: {{wallet}}, {{listing_id}}, {{request_id}}, {{service}})

##### `proxygate listings headers unset <listing-id> <name>`

Remove an upstream header

#### `proxygate listings test <id>`

Test endpoints for an existing listing

### `proxygate tunnel`

Expose local services to Proxygate via a reverse tunnel

- `-c, --config <path>` - Path to tunnel YAML config

### `proxygate settlements`

View settlement history (buyer spend or seller earnings)

- `-r, --role <role>` - Role: buyer or seller
- `-s, --service <name>` - Filter by service
- `--from <date>` - Start date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- `--to <date>` - End date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- `-l, --limit <n>` - Max entries

### `proxygate apis [search]`

Browse and search available API listings (no auth required)

Aliases: `search`

- `-q, --query <text>` - Search by name or description
- `-s, --service <slug>` - Filter by exact service slug
- `-c, --category <slug>` - Filter by category slug
- `--sort <order>` - Sort: price_asc, price_desc, popular, newest
- `-l, --limit <n>` - Max results
- `--cursor <id>` - Pagination cursor (listing ID from previous page)
- `--verified` - Show only verified sellers
- `--compact` - Minimal output: id, name, price only (good for agents)
- `-t, --type <type>` - Filter by listing type (skill, product, dataset, service, connector)

### `proxygate services`

View aggregated service stats (no auth required)

### `proxygate categories`

List API categories (no auth required)

### `proxygate rate`

Rate a seller after a proxy request

- `--request-id <id>` - Request ID from the proxy response receipt
- `--up` - Positive rating (thumbs up)
- `--down` - Negative rating (thumbs down)

### `proxygate verify-email`

Confirm your contact email with the one-time token from the email

- `--token <token>` - Verification token from the email

### `proxygate create [name]`

Scaffold a new Proxygate agent project

- `-t, --template <template>` - Template to use (http-api, llm-agent)
- `-p, --port <port>` - Local port
- `--price <price>` - Price per request in micro-cents

### `proxygate test`

Test local services defined in proxygate.tunnel.yaml

- `-c, --config <path>` - Path to tunnel YAML config
- `--payload <json>` - Custom JSON payload for POST endpoints
- `--endpoint <spec>` - Test a single endpoint (e.g., "POST /v1/analyze")

### `proxygate dev`

Start tunnel in dev mode with verbose logging and config watching

- `-c, --config <path>` - Path to tunnel YAML config

### `proxygate skills`

Manage agent skills for Proxygate

#### `proxygate skills install`

Install Proxygate skills for Claude Code and Codex CLI

- `--json` - JSON output

### `proxygate metadata`

Machine-readable project metadata (for AI agents and tooling)

### `proxygate commands`

Machine-readable command catalog with args, types, and schemas (for AI agents)

### `proxygate challenge`

$1 Agent Challenge commands

#### `proxygate challenge apply`

Apply for the $1 Agent Challenge (opens browser)

#### `proxygate challenge status`

Check your challenge application status

- `--challenge <slug>` - Challenge slug

### `proxygate login`

Authenticate with API key or wallet keypair

- `--key <apiKey>` - API key (starts with pg_live_)
- `--keypair <path>` - Path to keypair file
- `--generate` - Generate a new wallet keypair

### `proxygate logout`

Remove auth credentials from config

- `--all` - Remove both API key and keypair (requires confirmation)

### `proxygate whoami`

Show current auth status and configuration
